pattern: The three token shapes are prefix-distinguishable by their first character, so a single left-to-right scan can replace each token as it's recognized without ever needing to look ahead more than four characters.
hints: The string is only ever built from three fixed tokens: a lone letter, an empty-parens pair, and a three-letter word in parens.
Each of those three tokens maps to exactly one fixed replacement, so you never need to reason about their meaning beyond substitution.
Scan left to right: on 'G' emit 'G' and advance one; on '(' check the very next character to tell '()' from '(al)', emit 'o' or 'al', and advance past the whole token.

edge_inputs.description: a short mix of all three tokens
minimum length, a single empty-parens token
only (al) tokens back to back, easy to mis-split by character count
alternating tokens where a naive fixed-width scan would misalign

stress.description: long random command built from the three valid tokens
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    tokens = ['G', '()', '(al)']
    out = []
    length = 0
    limit = max(1, min(size, 100))
    while length < limit:
        t = rng.choice(tokens)
        if length + len(t) > 100:
            break
        out.append(t)
        length += len(t)
    if not out:
        out.append('G')
    return [''.join(out)]
